_comment: Cursor 3 hooks let you run scripts on agent events. This file is INTENTIONALLY MINIMAL — uncomment entries after deciding you want the friction. Each hook runs a shell command; keep them fast (< 2s) or they'll slow the loop.

_examples_disabled.description: Run typecheck after every TS/TSX edit. DISABLED by default — noisy on multi-file refactors.

_examples_disabled.command: echo 'Blocked by hooks.json — requires explicit user confirmation' && exit 1
_examples_disabled.description: Safety net for destructive commands. DISABLED — Cursor's built-in confirm covers most cases.

_examples_disabled.command: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const line = '\n- ' + new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) + ' | TODO | agent turn completed | —'; fs.appendFileSync('STATUS.md', line);"
_examples_disabled.description: Auto-append a TODO line to STATUS.md after every agent turn. DISABLED — prefer /status-append for curated entries.

_howTo: To enable an example: move it from `_examples_disabled` into a top-level `hooks` array. Hooks run in the project root. Windows users: commands run through PowerShell — wrap non-trivial shell syntax in a .ps1 or Node script under `.cursor/scripts/`.
